compiled_state.title: [red-test-writer + implementation-plan]: RED test commit blocked -> test-index RED marker not added by sub-agents
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

### Scenario Type
User-Flagged Behavior (host project session)

### Expected Behavior
When a red-test-writer sub-agent writes a RED test, it must also add a RED marker to `.test-index` (format: `[test_name] source_file.py`) before the test is committed. Without the RED marker, the pre-commit test gate blocks committing the failing test because the RED boundary isn't declared. Similarly, when /dso:implementation-plan creates a RED testing task, it should include the step to add the RED marker to `.test-index` as part of the task specification.

### Actual Behavior
The red-test-writer sub-agent wrote a failing test (src/components/DiffList.test.tsx) but did not add a RED marker to `.test-index`. When the session tried to commit the RED test alone, the pre-commit hook blocked it with a test failure (expected — RED tests can't pass without implementation). The session interpreted this as 'both tasks must be committed atomically' rather than 'add a RED marker to .test-index to allow committing the failing test independently.'

### Observed Log
```
git commit -m 'add red test for...'
Error: Exit code 1
> jest --ci --coverage -- DiffList.test.tsx
[RED test fails as expected]
⏺ The pre-commit hook blocks committing RED tests alone (expected - they can't pass without the implementation). Both tasks must be committed atomically.
```

### Root Cause Direction
1. The red-test-writer agent definition/prompt does not include instructions to add a RED marker to `.test-index` after writing the test
2. /dso:implementation-plan task descriptions for RED testing tasks do not specify adding the RED marker step
3. Sessions in host projects don't know the RED marker mechanism, causing them to misinterpret the pre-commit gate failure

### Fix Direction
1. Update the dso:red-test-writer agent to add the RED marker to `.test-index` after writing the RED test file
2. Update /dso:implementation-plan to include 'add [test_name] marker to .test-index' in RED task specifications
3. Consider adding a clearer error message in pre-commit-test-gate.sh when a failing test has no RED marker (to guide host project sessions)
